sections.content:

print(f"\nLevel: {current_frame.level}, Step: {current_frame.step}")

</parameter>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The output was truncated. Let me focus on the key entities - the blue agent and any green blocks. Let me filter for just those.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Filter for key entities: agent (blue/b) and green blocks (N/light green)
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
key_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] in ['b', 'N', 'S']]
print("Key entities:")
for n in key_nodes:
    # Get center from boundary
    b = n['boundary']
    r_avg = sum(p[0] for p in b) / len(b)
    c_avg = sum(p[1] for p in b) / len(b)
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} center~({r_avg:.0f},{c_avg:.0f})")

# Also check what happened between previous and current frame
if previous_frame:
    prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
    prev_nodes = prev_seg['nodes']
    prev_key = {n['color']: n for n in prev_nodes if n['color'] in ['b', 'N']}
    curr_key = {n['color']: n for n in nodes if n['color'] in ['b', 'N']}
    print(f"\nPrevious key entities: {[(k, v['pixels']) for k,v in prev_key.items()]}")
    print(f"Current key entities: {[(k, v['pixels']) for k,v in curr_key.items()]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check if level is done or if there are remaining targets
# Look for any non-background, non-agent colored objects
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
# Filter out the big background objects (black B, sky blue S) and small gray dots (G)
important = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] not in ['B', 'S', 'G'] and n['pixels'] > 1]
print("Important remaining objects:")
for n in important:
    b = n['boundary']
    r_avg = sum(p[0] for p in b) / len(b)
    c_avg = sum(p[1] for p in b) / len(b)
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} center~({r_avg:.0f},{c_avg:.0f})")

print(f"\nLevel: {current_frame.level}, Step: {current_frame.step}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}, Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
